titleStmt.title: SII 23.113: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 113. (A.R. No. 113 of 1907.) Kalliḍaikkuṟichchi, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District Kulaśēkharamuḍaiyār temple—on the wall at the entrance

summary.p: This is dated in Śaka 1480, Kollam 73[4], Puraṭṭādi 3, śu. 1, Friday, Uttiraṭṭādi, corresponding probably to A.D. 1558, September 2 (but it was ba. 4, Bharaṇi), without any reference to the reigning king of the period (Sadāśivarāya). It registers an order of Vīrappa-Nāyakkar-Ayyaṉ evidently a petty ruler of this region, communicated to Kurukularāyar the maṇiyam of Kallaḍaikkuṟichchi and its environs and to the nāṭṭār (assembly), exempting the masons and the carpenters of Añjupati (lit. five places) from the payment of the taxes vari and iṟai (probably due on their profession and income). This Vīrappa-Nāyaka seems to be different from his namesake the son of the Madura Nāyaka chief Viśvanātha-Kṛishṇappa mentioned in No. 35 of 1908 dated in Śaka 1505.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . cakāptam 1000 4 100 8 10 ṉ
mel cellāniṉṟa kollam 7 10 24 varuṣam puraṭṭā
timātam 3 tiyati pūrvapakṣattu pratamaiyum ve
ḷḷiyāṭcaiyum peṟṟa Uttiraṭṭātināḷ ceyta Uṟuti piṭipāṭuāvitu ciṟimatu
virappaṉāyakkar Ayyaṉ tiruvuḷḷampaṟṟiaruḷi kallaṭaikkuṟicci Uḷ
ḷiṭṭa paṟṟu maṇiyam kurukularāyarum nāṭṭārum kūṭi kaṟpitta kāriyam
meṟpaṭi Ūrom Añcupatiyil kollaṟkum taiccaṟkum Iṉṉāḷ muta
lukku Ivarkaḷukku Uṇṭāṉa variyum Iṟaiyum niṅkallāka cukattile Iruk
kumpaṭiyum kaṭṭaḷaiiṭṭu Iṉṉāḷmutalukkuk kallilum cempilum veṭ
ṭi Ācantiratāravaṟ cantatippiṟavecame Intappaṭikku naṭatti varumpaṭi
kaṟpittu kalveṭṭivittuk kuṭuttom Itukku Iyātāmoruvaṉ
viṟkaṉaṅkaḷ paṇṇuvārkaḷ keṅkaikkaraiyil kārāmpacuvai
k koṉṟa toṣattil pokakkaṭavāṉākavum Ippaṭi kaṟpittu Uṟu
tipiṭipāṭāka kalveṭṭivittamaikku Ippaṭi kurukularāyar Eḻuttu .
Digital edition of SII 23.113 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
